title: Manager, GTM Operations (RevOps)
description: Iru is hiring a Manager, GTM Operations to lead its RevOps function—the team responsible for the systems, data, and processes that keep the revenue engine accurate, predictable, and scalable. The role supports Sales, SDR, Customer Success, Solutions Engineering, and Marketing, owning marketing operations (lifecycle, scoring, attribution), CS operations, and operational sales planning. This is a player-coach role with one direct report. Requires 5–8 years in Revenue/GTM Operations in B2B SaaS, deep HubSpot and/or Salesforce proficiency, and BI tool experience.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years exceeds 5-8 year requirement, strong HubSpot/Salesforce admin background, direct RevOps expertise across sales/marketing/CS operations, player-coach leadership style aligns perfectly with the one-direct-report structure.
ai_opener: Hi there—I came across Iru's GTM Operations role and it immediately resonated. I've spent 15+ years building scalable RevOps functions in B2B SaaS, with deep expertise in exactly what you're looking for: HubSpot/Salesforce architecture, marketing lifecycle & attribution, CS operations, and pipeline forecasting. I'd love to explore how I can help you build a predictable, data-driven revenue engine.

title: Staff Sales Operations Manager
description: Chainguard seeks a Staff Sales Operations Manager combining strategic vision with operational rigor and a track record of building scalable processes in high-growth B2B SaaS. Responsibilities include owning the operational rhythm (forecast calls, pipeline reviews), territory strategy, account assignment frameworks, and implementing automation across the territory management lifecycle. Requires 6–10+ years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy with 2+ years people management, advanced Salesforce proficiency, and strong analytical skills including SQL and BI platforms like Sigma, Tableau, or Looker.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's senior RevOps/GTM background, strategic focus on scalable processes, and Salesforce expertise. Strong match on forecast/pipeline rigor and people leadership; minor gap if SQL/BI platform depth is limited, but core competencies are well-covered.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building revenue operations engines in high-growth B2B SaaS, and Chainguard's focus on territory strategy and operational rigor resonates deeply with my work scaling pipeline forecasting and territory frameworks. Your Staff Sales Operations role looks like exactly the kind of strategic operations challenge I thrive in, particularly the emphasis on building scalable processes across the GTM motion.

title: Sales Operations Manager
description: AssetWatch, a remote-first company, is hiring a Sales Operations Manager to support sales forecasting, pipeline management, and territory planning. The role manages cross-functional operational initiatives and projects. Requires 4–7 years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or similar within B2B SaaS, strong Salesforce reporting and CRM operational experience, and advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills. Familiarity with modern GTM tools such as Gong, Outreach, ZoomInfo, and AI automation tools like Clay or Zapier is a strong plus.

However, the 4-7 year requirement and Sales Operations Manager title suggest a more specialized, potentially narrower scope than Omri's senior executive background, which may indicate overqualification for the level.

With 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps and deep Salesforce/GTM stack experience (Gong, Outreach, ZoomInfo), I've scaled similar operations across multiple growth phases. I'd be interested in exploring how my background in cross-functional revenue systems could accelerate your operational maturity.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: VulnCheck, a fast-growing cybersecurity company, is hiring a Revenue Operations Manager to support the operational engine powering its go-to-market teams. The role sits at the intersection of Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Finance—owning CRM integrity, optimizing revenue workflows, building reporting infrastructure, and generating leadership insights. title: Revenue Operations Manager (Sales & Customer Experience)
description: Madison Logic is hiring a Revenue Operations Manager to document and optimize post-sale workflows (onboarding, EBRs, renewals, and expansion motions) in partnership with CS and Delivery teams. The role involves creating customer health scoring frameworks and partnering with CS leadership on coverage models, capacity planning, and segmentation. Requires 5–7 years in Sales Operations, Customer Success Operations, or Revenue Operations (B2B SaaS preferred), strong Salesforce power-user skills, and a track record of using AI and automation to improve GTM performance.
score_reason: Strong alignment on RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS background, and Salesforce proficiency. Slight concern: role is heavily CS/post-sale focused rather than full GTM pipeline, and 15+ years may position Omri above this Manager-level scope.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, is hiring a GTM Engineer as it scales rapidly in 2026. The role replaces patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered automations—including lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, and onboarding flows. The ideal candidate can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and AI agents that make the GTM team highly effective, eliminating hours of manual work every week.

However, the role's heavy coding/engineering focus and AI agent development is a meaningful shift from traditional RevOps, requiring hands-on development skills that aren't confirmed in the profile.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Conveo's GTM Engineer role and immediately thought of you—your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue operations puts you in the rare position to actually own the problems this role solves: lead scoring, attribution, onboarding automation. Most GTM teams stay stuck in manual workflows because they lack someone who speaks both operations *and* systems. Conveo's bet on purpose-built AI agents for revenue teams feels like exactly where the space is heading.
